module.title: The Watch at Crow's Hollow
module.author: Lantern & Blade Project
module.description: A derelict Warden watch-house on the Hollow Edge. For three nights running, the lanterns along its curtain wall have relit themselves at dusk and burned until dawn. The nearest village sent a child to investigate; the child did not come back. A short dungeon in six rooms, climaxing in the Warden-Lieutenant's crypt. Designed for a level 3 character operating solo.

watch_house_yard.name: The Watch-House Yard
watch_house_yard.description: The road from Thornford's Common runs out of the woods and right up to the yard's low stone wall, as if it were still an imperial thoroughfare. Beyond the wall: a flagged courtyard strangled with dry grass, a half-collapsed stable, and the watch-house itself — two stories of squat grey limestone with a peaked slate roof. Above the oaken door, a carved crest: a stylized lantern encircled by a chain of laurels. Above the crest, set into sconces along the curtain wall, four iron watch-lanterns burn with a thin, steady, unshielded flame. It is mid-afternoon. There is no wind to speak of. The lanterns are lit anyway.

environment.name: Unquiet Stillness
environment.description: No birds. No insects. The sound of the player's own breath is audible. The lanterns do not flicker.
effects: Sound carries further than it should — the slightest noise from inside the watch-house is likely to be heard.

features.name: The Warden Crest
features.description: A single large stone carved above the oaken door: a lantern wrapped in a chain of laurels. Beneath it, in small deeply-cut script, the Watch's motto — WE KEEP THE LIGHT SO THE LIGHT KEEPS US.
features.on_examine: The laurels are not a Dawnlight convention — they are older, pre-imperial, and the Wardens borrowed them from someone who mattered to the pre-imperial cults. The crest predates the watch-house itself; it's been moved here from somewhere else. Someone is buried under the stone the crest is carved into, or was.
features.gm_notes: Players bearing the Rubbing of the Lantern Tree will recognize the motto instantly. Players bearing the Lanternblade will see the blade's runes warm slightly against the crest.

features.name: The Burning Watch-Lanterns
features.description: Four iron watch-lanterns along the curtain wall, set in bracketed sconces at roughly head height. Each is burning with a steady, pale, unshielded flame about the size of a thumb. There is no oil in them. There is no wick.
features.on_examine: The flames do not respond to breath. A palm brought near one feels warmth, but the warmth does not spread — it is present exactly in the flame and nowhere else. This is proper Warden lantern-work behaving the way Warden lantern-work is supposed to behave. Something inside the watch-house has relit them.
features.gm_notes: The lanterns are a breadcrumb: whatever is in the crypt is waking, and the old lantern-work is responding. Do not let the player extinguish them — they cannot be put out from outside.

gate_room.name: The Gate Room
gate_room.description: The oaken door opens on a high-ceilinged guardroom paved in slate, cold enough that the player's breath shows. A long wooden counter runs along the right wall, its surface worn smooth by a thousand signed duty-rosters. Above it, pegged to a plank, a Warden muster-roll. Two doorways lead off to the left and right; a third, directly ahead, is an iron-strapped door set deeper into the wall than seems structurally necessary. Everything is coated with the fine grey dust of sixty years of emptiness — except a single line of bootprints, fresh, small, leading inward from the threshold and vanishing at the rightmost doorway.

the iron-strapped door.reveal_hint: The iron-strapped door will not open. Its bar-latch is warded with an old lantern-sigil — it is not a mechanical lock.

environment.name: Old Cold
environment.description: The room holds the specific deep chill of a cellar that has not been warmed in decades. Breath shows. Metal is painful to touch ungloved.
effects: Prolonged bare contact with metal risks numb fingers; a GM may call for a Constitution save against extended exposure.

features.name: The Muster-Roll
features.description: A split oak plank pegged to the wall above the counter, roughly two feet wide, bearing a list of fourteen names in the neat column-hand of a Warden quartermaster. The top of the plank reads CROW'S HOLLOW GARRISON · WINTER OF THE SIXTY-THIRD YEAR. Beside each name, a small inked symbol: fourteen of them match — a laurel. One name, second from the top — LT. ORICK HAVEL — has no laurel beside it. Instead, a small circle with a line through it.
features.on_examine: A character with Warden lore or who has read any Warden document before will know: the laurel means 'returned home properly' — a Warden who died was laid to rest under their own name. The circle-with-a-line is the older mark — 'returned, but not properly.' Lt. Orick Havel, whoever he was, is somewhere in this watch-house, and the Watch itself knew he had not been put away correctly.
features.gm_notes: First major clue. This is the player's introduction to the Warden-Lieutenant as a named figure with an unfinished problem.

features.name: Overturned Equipment Locker
features.description: A tall wooden locker behind the counter has been pulled from the wall and dumped on its side. The Warden quartermaster's seal — a small waxy lantern stamp — has been broken off the latch, evidently very recently. Most of what it held is scattered: mothballed gambesons, a pair of broken belt-hooks, and a surprising quantity of grey paper envelopes sealed with a cord.
features.on_success: Among the scattered envelopes is a small oilcloth-wrapped bundle that escaped the thieves' notice: two sealed vials, a folded paper bearing a hand-drawn diagram, and a few iron nails.
features.on_failure: The locker is mostly empty; someone has been through it already.
features.gm_notes: Whoever broke the quartermaster's seal is the same party whose bootprints lead into the officer's study. Foreshadow without naming.

hazards.name: Rotten Floorboard
hazards.description: A single wide board of the slate flooring is, in fact, not slate — it is a rotten plank painted grey to match. It covers an old drainage pit that the original garrison used for washing blood off the slate after a fight.
hazards.gm_notes: Easy detect, medium avoidance. A player who finds it may use the pit tactically later (dragging an enemy in, hiding).

detection.on_success: One of the slate flags rings hollow under the foot. Closer, it's plainly a painted plank. Below: a rectangular pit, maybe four feet deep, dry but for old brown staining.

avoidance.on_success: You spread your weight across the adjacent flags and cross the plank without putting full weight on it.

on_failure.narration: The plank gives all at once, with the sound of a dry branch snapping, and drops you four feet down into a cold stone-walled pit. You land hard on an ankle.

reward_on_detection.narration: You keep your footing and note the pit for later.

module_bestiary.name: Crow's Hollow — Module Foes
module_bestiary.description: Unique foes specific to The Watch at Crow's Hollow.
